name: ADC Therapeutics SA
business: ADC Therapeutics is a commercial-stage global pioneer in the field of antibody drug conjugates (ADCs), with ZYNLONTA (loncastuximab tesirine-lpyl) approved for relapsed or refractory DLBCL after two or more lines of systemic therapy. They are pursuing expansion of ZYNLONTA into earlier lines of DLBCL and indolent lymphomas through clinical trials, and have a preclinical PSMA-targeting ADC in development.
sellsTo: Healthcare providers (academic and community settings) via wholesale distributors
Strategic partners (TPC in Japan, Sobi in certain territories, Overland ADCT BioPharma in greater China and Singapore)

exposures.sentence: ZYNLONTA (loncastuximab tesirine-lpyl), a CD19-directed ADC, received accelerated approval from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) and conditional approval from the European Commission, China National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) and Health Canada for the treatment of relapsed or refractory DLBCL after two or more lines of systemic therapy.
We have completed IND-enabling activities for ADCT-241, our next-generation PSMA-targeting ADC, which utilizes a differentiated exatecan-based payload with a novel hydrophilic linker.

catalysts.event: LOTIS-5 Phase 3 confirmatory trial topline data
catalysts.sentence: We expect to provide topline data in the first half of 2026 from the LOTIS-5 Phase 3 confirmatory trial of ZYNLONTA in combination with rituximab in patients with 2L+ DLBCL once the pre-specified number of approximately 262 progression-free survival events is reached and data are available.
Publication of the full results is anticipated by the end of 2026.

Enrollment in the LOTIS-7 clinical trial is ongoing, with complete enrollment of approximately 100 patients at the selected 150 g/kg dose of ZYNLONTA plus the approved dose of glofitamab expected during the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: LOTIS-7 full data sharing and publication
catalysts.sentence: We plan to share full data at a medical meeting and submit for publication by the end of 2026.

Assuming positive results, a supplemental Biologics License Application (sBLA) submission to the FDA will follow, with potential compendia inclusion in the first half of 2027 and confirmatory approval in 2L+ DLBCL in mid-2027.

Assuming positive results, a supplemental Biologics License Application (sBLA) submission to the FDA will follow, with potential compendia inclusion in the first half of 2027 and confirmatory approval in 2L+ DLBCL in mid-2027.
We anticipate publication of data as early as the end of 2026, and assuming positive data, we plan to assess regulatory and compendia strategies.
